title: OpenClaw gRPC deadline exceeded on inference service causes cascading upstream timeouts

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63017.

task.summary: Diagnose why upstream services are timing out despite the inference backend completing requests successfully.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm gRPC deadline propagation as the root cause, localize it to the misconfigured deadline on the client stub, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This is an ops-only synthetic incident derived from a real GitHub issue.

visible_context.issue_text: API callers report intermittent timeout errors on long-running inference requests. The inference service logs show requests completing in ~8s, but the gateway is cancelling them after 5s and returning DEADLINE_EXCEEDED to clients.
error_logs: inference-gateway: gRPC status=DEADLINE_EXCEEDED for request_id=ff291 after 5000ms
inference-service: request ff291 completed successfully in 8210ms
gateway: upstream call failed — propagating 504 to client
reproduction_steps: Inspect the gRPC deadline configuration on the client stub vs actual inference latency.
Confirm the gateway is cancelling in-flight requests before the backend finishes.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair is to increase the gRPC deadline on the client stub to accommodate p99 inference latency, or make it configurable.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident for a hardcoded gRPC deadline shorter than actual backend latency causing cascading timeouts.

constraints.notes: Ops-only smoke scenario. The standalone executor remains synthetic.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63017.
